{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/4775","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/4775/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/4775/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/4775/events","html_url":"https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/4775","id":818848041,"node_id":"MDU6SXNzdWU4MTg4NDgwNDE=","number":4775,"title":"Pinned memory leak","user":{"login":"nizamov","id":12709452,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyNzA5NDUy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/12709452?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nizamov","html_url":"https://github.com/nizamov","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nizamov/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nizamov/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nizamov/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nizamov/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nizamov/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nizamov/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nizamov/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nizamov/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nizamov/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":1360743887,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMzYwNzQzODg3","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/labels/issue-checked","name":"issue-checked","color":"ffffff","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2021-03-01T13:05:43Z","updated_at":"2024-10-06T08:01:24Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hi, there

seems that I have found some kind of a strange memory allocation bug. I am running Debian testing, which hast cuda 11.2 and cython 29.21. So I had to build cupy ("pip3 install cupy" - no errors reported except missing cudnn/cublas etc).

Now, the problem is that I wanted to benchmark memory transfer between host memory and device memory. In general, this is nothing more than measuring the timing of many transfers of the same array. So memory allocation had to be really negligible, but with cupy it is not. It is growing until it gets killed by OOM-killer. I had the similar test in Numba without any problems. 

Here is a simplified code that triggers such behavior on my PC (quite an old Xeon E3-1220 with 16Gb DDR3 memory, python 3.9.1).  If there is no explicit release of memory buffers, the memory occupied by python grows steadily. Actually it should not. I have added a small time delay to the cycle to be able to observe this behavior and found another strange thing. If time delay is longer than 0.2s (I suspect that the value depends on the hardware), then the memory occupation gets capped at 3.4 Gb and test passes. For comparison, with pinned_mempool.free_all_blocks() memory occupation remains at the level around 2.4Gb. 

It seems to me, that there is some automatic garbage collection which can not cope with a rate that this test allocates/deallocates pinned memory. But works to at least some extent, if the rate is decreased by addition of sufficient sleeping time. Since I do not observe such problem with Numba on the same configuration, I guess that this bug is not in linux/python/cuda stack but rather in cupy itself.  



```
import numpy as np
import cupy as cp
import time

N = 10*1024*1024

cpu_data = np.zeros(N, dtype=np.float32)

mempool = cp.get_default_memory_pool()
pinned_mempool = cp.get_default_pinned_memory_pool()

for i in range(5000):
    gpu_data = cp.asarray(cpu_data)

    if i % 100 == 0:
        print(i, mempool.used_bytes(), mempool.total_bytes(), pinned_mempool.n_free_blocks())    

        time.sleep(0.1)
        #pinned_mempool.free_all_blocks()

print("Ok")

```

Message in dmesg when test gets killed:

```
Out of memory: Killed process 7203 (python3) total-vm:17809796kB, anon-rss:115924kB, file-rss:75444kB, shmem-rss:11151360kB, pgtables:22756kB oom_score_adj:0
oom_reaper: reaped process 7203 (python3), now anon-rss:0kB, file-rss:74732kB, shmem-rss:11151360kB

```


Output of `python -c 'import cupy; cupy.show_config()'`

My code includes a lot of cpu/gpu transfers and the host memory consumption is huge, to the point that the system crashes. Also, as already noted, ulimit doesn't work as expected. Is there currently any workaround for this issue? Manually freeing pinned memory is the way to go?
